content: Using “AI for brainstorming ideas and concepts” so in short: yes, the game is using or used AI for creating art. Am I right?

Obviously not. I believe the full explanation has already made things quite clear. The current situation is just like this: in a handful of late-stage design cases, we tried using AI tools to explore certain issues or gather information—mainly to get reference images that are more creative or faster than traditional search engines. However, defining the entire game's art as "AI-generated" because of this is, in my opinion, a very biased and misleading conclusion. We will not respond further to questions of this nature.

Thanks for your responses! Some Vietnamese players have really terrible English literacy per se, so they have trouble reading overly long English paragraphs, so my apology on their behalf over them misunderstanding your explainations, and sorry for the trouble as well
P.S: Only a few troublemakers have this tendercy, the majority of Vietnamese community can understand your answer very well
I don’t say the whole game, I just want to confirm if you’re using AI for creating images which are later be used in any ways to make the final illustrations in game.

Wow, there is sth about this guy reading comprehension that renders me speechless. Seems like they already have a bias in their head and just looking for opportunities to strengthen that bias.
I will said once again, learn the process of making art before speaking about art.
you seems like a Casiah fan
perharps they used Chat GPT to translate

<@364412673275723779> to me, i don’t mind if the game use AI. But if they used, I want to know how much as well how they used it to make art
I think everyone has the right to know that and OP’s concern is not absurd
They literally just said the extent is using it to create reference images that are then used by artists and that's it
There you go, there's your answer

probably a hater

i don't think it's an absurd question, since as op said, it's a question that's frequently asked as well, but please understand that this is an especially sensitive topic for artists

pressing people and asking if their work used any ai is akin to pointing out that their work is illegitimate or "lesser", even if it may not seem that way to you

I found “reference images” is rather vague words. How are those reference images are used? They just get the ideas from them or they  paint over it and refine until they’re satisfied? All I see is everyone assaults anyone who thinks the game may have used AI. AI is just a tool, in the end it’s still humans that are creating.

My problem is the way people keep saying this or that is AI and hope people will say sth

pls learn before you speak

that guy mindset is certainly sth

I can't be bothered anymore, they gonna believe what they want to believe and I can't be assed to preach to the inconvertible.

> It’s true that, with the rise of AI technology and out of a need to keep up with technical advancements, we began to experiment in 2023 with AI for brainstorming or idea refinement, aiming to boost efficiency during concept discussions. However, the amount of AI-assisted content in the game is extremely limited; even when used, human artists remain absolutely central to all creative work.
> 
> Personally, I believe AI still has many shortcomings that can’t replace human artists, whether in design, expression, or collaborative execution. That may change in the future. If the day ever comes when we use AI to generate official content, we will absolutely disclose it to players—openly and clearly—just as we do with AI translation, and never secretly.
Yep, something something. Reading comprehension is a dying skill
pressing on the topic any further is frankly, really rude
Reading issue <:pray:1356053116260454534>

let's stop here please

if it feels vague to you still, please just understand that this topic makes people uncomfortable, and the devs have spoken that they will not respond any more
Tawil not opened all boundaries for this

when ppl try to tell him the truth , he just ignore it and think they assualt him . Cant accept the truth

i think you have a point when you say that 'ai is just a tool, in the end it's still humans that are creating' and it is true that there is a unique set of skills that are needed to make appealing ai illustrations like having a discerning eye or good prompt-writing skills but the important thing is that the appeal for artists of this genre is the effort of drawing and physically making the illustrations, that's the reason why there are certain artists like ask and liduke who are recognized and their characters noteworthy, it's not just because their art is visually appealing but because people can recognize the time and effort spent honing their ability to reach the point where they can make that style. from the perspective of a consumer, having concerns for ai art can be justified by not wanting to support ai tools overtaking artists or various other concerns but if you flip your mindset and look at it from the artists' view then there are also few reasons to use ai art in any meaningful (meaningful in this situation refers to being heavily involved in the final draft process) way when producing a work-related illustration. because of that, the phrase 'reference image' in light's response is not specific and it can be scrutinized heavily but looking at the grand picture i think it's not necessary to try to find the exact meaning since it doesn't impact the final product in a meaningful way (the picture is already drawn after all) and there's nothing for biav to gain from being duplicitous

is this your first time learning that artists in fact use references in making art? referencing is **not** copying. they simply used AI to get a specific idea, a **reference**, to see an image of their vision so the artists that works with BIAV can get a clear view of what the devs want to be created. they wanted to gather an inspiration faster because all you have to do is input a few words and the AI can generate a reference for visual guidance. you're treating this as if they used AI art, not by their own hands. all the creative decisions, execution, and interpretation are done by the hard working artists of BIAV, not AI.

it feels like you are already biased and convinced that they are using AI art, nitpicking Light's explanation and only focused on "we tried using AI tools to explore" and doesn't read the rest of it. so all of this explanation is useless, it's like we're all talking to a brick wall <:idk:1356084138280489080>

Reference image refers to an image you put on the side while drawing as an example. Whether you take a pose, colour pallete, or a detail/patern and redo it in your own way.

An example here is once again, Nautilas bike having the patern of an actual nautilus shell. 

The artists didn't just make the pattern out of nowhere. They Googled an image of the shell probably and used it as inspiration.

so in essence it's literally stating that ai is not in the final game.

A lot of artist or even concept designers already when brainstorming ideas, just Google images and stich them together.

Most early start environment artists do this.
You've got no clue how many 2001 space odyssey obelisks I've seen in behind close door concept environments.
tbh normal ppl doesn't need to know that much, they only need some reading and common sense to conclude that this game doesn't use AI
You... expect way too much on people. The silent majority of people who don't even participate or not care about the discussion... well don't see when something is ai... or outright don't care.
Again, once you try to say something is ai. You already in my eyes signed the sentence that you should know at least a bit of the process of making digital art.
then again when they've already dead set on their agenda, admitting that they were wrong will be the last thing they do

I've been working and known a lot of bg artists and charadesigners, they always use references, some are even using what's called "photobashing" and it's not harming the industry artists like AI does. 
Some people should know the process a little more before doubting and accusing early.

<@452516689225973761> thank you for explanation. From what i know, people can use AI generated image, re-touch/ change it, refill it to AI to get new and repeat it until they’re ok. I just don’t know if morimens as same or not. But like Jynn said, it’s rude question from start, so I will stop asking it and sorry Light-Cat if it’s hurt his feelings. From his answer, i understand that they’re used AI, but not what I think

it's all cool. Remember you can be against AI art, you can be for artists. The best way to support artists will always be just... giving the artists you like support, whether its commissions, money, or just liking their tweets and sharing the art you like. It's a tough task telling whats ai and whats not for the common lad or lass, so it's always good to stick to methods of support that... ya-know actually bring meaning to em.
scream at big corporations who listen to silicon valley dimwits who advertise AI as all powerful.
as in like... only rally when a lot, a lot of people have already confirmed something is ai

the questions in themselves are not exactly rude and it's valid most of the time as well, i think it's just continuing it after an answer has been given (which is what i wanted to stop in the first place) is the rude-ish part as well as how you worded things... but

i also think there's been some miscommunication (your english comprehension needs some work, but it's okay) here and there as well - referencing art isn't the same as generating art, and if taken in a vacuum your questions aren't exactly accusing studio biav or anyone of anything (in fact, you were the one that mentioned calling the devs' attention, which is what should've been done in the first place)

thanks for being polite though despite everything <:goodjob2:1356053078738206840> 

personal note:
||some people here are just... idk, seeing their hateful behavior makes me wanna frequent here less. i understand defending the integrity of the game we all love but let's not use it as an avenue to start throwing malicious comments onto people. this forum channel is for questions and answers, not calling others fake fans of the game or other discrediting or derogatory remarks.||
